key_event.1885-1894: British Raj consolidated; Indian National Congress founded (1885)
key_event.1895-1904: Curzon centralization and coercive famine/plague management
key_event.1905-1914: Swadeshi movement and Bengal partition backlash
key_event.1915-1924: Gandhi's mass Non-Cooperation Movement transforms Congress into mass politics
key_event.1925-1934: Salt March / Civil Disobedience and Purna Swaraj declaration
key_event.1935-1944: Wartime state expansion, Quit India repression, Bengal Famine
key_event.1945-1954: Independence, Constitution, and first universal-franchise elections
key_event.1955-1964: Nehruvian state-led industrialization and permit-license Raj expansion
key_event.1965-1974: Indira Gandhi's personalization of power amid rising mobilization
key_event.1975-1984: Emergency then 1977 electoral ouster; Punjab insurgency
key_event.1985-1994: Babri Masjid demolition; Mandal mobilization; 1991 liberalization
key_event.1995-2004: Liberalization, IT boom, PIL/RTI, and coalition-era societal mobilization
key_event.2005-2014: RTI, rights-based laws, and anti-corruption mass mobilization
key_event.2015-2024: BJP centralization and digital state vs. periodic societal pushback
